facts: Bilateral elongated styloid processes extend down from the skull base.
Usually asymptomatic; can cause throat pain, ear pain, swallowing difficulty, or a foreign-body sensation.
Managed by observation if asymptomatic; surgery only when symptoms are severe.
DZStLYBgRAE.breakdown:
Answer: Eagle Syndrome

👀 What you see:
Bilateral elongated styloid processes extending downward from the skull base.

🦴 Why it matters:
Usually asymptomatic but can cause throat pain, ear pain, swallowing difficulty, or a foreign body sensation.

💊 Treatment:
Observation if asymptomatic. Surgery may be needed if symptoms are severe.

📝 Takeaway:
Extra-long styloid processes = Eagle syndrome.
